ogTitle: Smeaton vs Smiths Gully: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Smeaton and Smiths Gully.
heading: Smeaton vs Smiths Gully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Smeaton, Victoria and Smiths Gully, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Smeaton has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Smiths Gully (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Smeaton or Smiths Gully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Smeaton has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Smiths Gully scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Smeaton scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Smiths Gully scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Smeaton or Smiths Gully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Smeaton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
